enrichment.short_description: Document management system with a free open-source Community Edition and paid Business/Enterprise editions.
enrichment.starting_price: Free (Community Edition); custom pricing for paid editions
enrichment.seo_title: LogicalDOC Review 2026: Features, Pricing and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: LogicalDOC is a document management system with a free open-source edition and paid Enterprise tiers. Read our 2026 review of features and pricing.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: LogicalDOC is a document management system developed by LogicalDOC Srl, an Italian software company founded in 2006 and headquartered in Carpi, in the province of Modena, Italy. Since its founding, the company has expanded internationally, maintaining offices and multilingual support operations across Italy, the United States, Germany, Spain, France, and Portugal, and serving enterprise and mid-market clients globally rather than remaining a purely regional Italian vendor. The product's defining characteristic is its dual-edition model: LogicalDOC Community Edition (CE) is a genuinely free and open-source document management system, released under the GNU Lesser General Public License version 3.0 (LGPL v3), with full source code available on GitHub, giving smaller organizations and technically capable teams a no-cost path into structured document management, version control, and basic workflow. Layered on top of the free Community Edition, LogicalDOC offers Business and Enterprise editions that add capabilities aimed at larger, more complex organizations, including clustering for high availability, more advanced workflow automation, deeper records management functionality, electronic signature integration, and commercial support contracts, positioning LogicalDOC Enterprise as, in the company's own words, its 'most comprehensive product' with a modular, flexible architecture. Across all editions, the platform is cross-platform (running on Windows, Linux, and macOS server environments), and the feature set spans core document management (storage, versioning, metadata, full-text search), a configurable workflow engine for routing documents through review and approval cycles, collaboration tools for distributed and remote teams, electronic signature and online forms capability, and dedicated mobile apps for accessing documents away from a desktop. Beyond the on-premises Community and Enterprise editions, LogicalDOC also offers cloud and hybrid-cloud deployment options for organizations that prefer a managed or partially managed hosting model, as well as specialized packaged versions built to run on QNAP and Synology network-attached storage devices, which is a somewhat distinctive go-to-market move that targets small businesses and IT departments that already run NAS infrastructure and want to add document management without provisioning a separate server. LogicalDOC's pricing is not transparently published for the paid tiers: the company's pricing pages route prospective business and enterprise customers to a sales contact form rather than listing fixed per-user or per-month prices, which is common in the document management and ECM (enterprise content management) space but means buyers must go through a sales conversation to get a firm quote; the SaaS/cloud edition is described as being priced based on a combination of storage needs and user count, customized per customer. The Community Edition, by contrast, remains genuinely free with no artificial user or storage caps beyond what the underlying open-source software and self-hosted infrastructure can support. LogicalDOC's target audience spans a range of regulated and document-heavy industries, including healthcare organizations needing compliant records handling, legal firms managing case files and contracts, construction companies coordinating drawings and project documentation, research institutions, sales and marketing departments needing collateral management, and government agencies with formal records-retention and compliance requirements; the company has also received industry recognition, including a 'Best Document Control Software' award from Digital.com in 2021 and a SourceForge Top Performer award in document management in 2026. LogicalDOC's competitive set includes both open-source alternatives, such as OpenKM, Alfresco Community Edition, and Nuxeo, and commercial document/content management platforms including M-Files, DocuWare, SharePoint, Box, and OpenText. LogicalDOC differentiates itself primarily through its genuinely free, fully open-source Community Edition, LGPL-licensed, source-available, with no feature timer or forced upgrade, combined with a credible enterprise-grade upgrade path (clustering, advanced workflow, records management, commercial support) for organizations that outgrow the free tier, giving buyers a low-risk way to trial and adopt the platform before committing budget to a paid license, which is a less common combination than either fully proprietary DMS vendors or open-source projects with weaker enterprise features.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: LogicalDOC is a document management system built by LogicalDOC Srl, an Italian company founded in 2006 and headquartered in Carpi, Italy, with additional offices across the US, Germany, Spain, France, and Portugal. It is distinguished by offering a genuinely free, open-source Community Edition alongside paid Business and Enterprise editions for larger organizations.

The platform covers document storage, versioning, full-text search, workflow automation, electronic signatures, and mobile access, and is available in on-premises, cloud/hybrid-cloud, and specialized NAS-appliance (QNAP/Synology) deployment forms.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Core capabilities include document versioning and metadata management, a configurable workflow engine for review and approval routing, electronic signature and online forms, and dedicated mobile apps for remote document access.

The Enterprise edition adds clustering for high availability, more advanced workflow automation, deeper records management, and commercial support, while a genuinely open-source LGPL v3 Community Edition remains free with full source code available.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: LogicalDOC Community Edition is free and open source with no license cost. Business and Enterprise edition pricing is not publicly listed; prospective customers must contact LogicalDOC's sales team for a custom quote based on user count, deployment model, and required features.
The cloud/SaaS edition is priced based on a combination of storage needs and number of users, customized per customer rather than following a fixed published price list.

features.title: Document versioning and metadata
features.description: Tracks document revisions with structured metadata and full-text search.

features.title: Workflow automation engine
features.description: Configurable routing for document review, approval, and circulation processes.

features.title: Electronic signature and online forms
features.description: Built-in e-signature capability and web-based form handling.

features.title: Records management
features.description: Enterprise-tier functionality for formal, compliance-oriented records retention.

features.title: Clustering and high availability
features.description: Enterprise-tier clustering support for larger, mission-critical deployments.

features.title: Cross-platform and NAS deployment
features.description: Runs on Windows, Linux, and macOS, plus specialized editions for QNAP and Synology NAS devices.

features.title: Mobile access
features.description: Dedicated mobile apps for accessing and managing documents away from a desktop.

features.title: Cloud and hybrid-cloud options
features.description: Managed cloud deployment alongside the traditional on-premises Community/Enterprise install.

pros: Genuinely free, open-source Community Edition with full source code (LGPL v3)
Clear upgrade path from free CE to feature-rich Enterprise edition
Long operating history since 2006 with established multi-country presence
Distinctive NAS-appliance editions (QNAP, Synology) for SMBs already using that infrastructure
Broad platform support (Windows, Linux, macOS) and cloud/hybrid deployment options
cons: Business and Enterprise pricing is not publicly disclosed, requiring a sales conversation
Community Edition lacks clustering, advanced workflow, and commercial support found in paid tiers
Smaller brand recognition than larger DMS/ECM players like SharePoint, Box, or OpenText
Self-hosting the Community Edition requires in-house IT/server expertise
UI and modernization pace may lag newer cloud-native document management entrants

faqs.question: Is LogicalDOC free?
faqs.answer: LogicalDOC's Community Edition is completely free and open source under the LGPL v3 license. Business and Enterprise editions are paid and require contacting sales for pricing.

faqs.question: When was LogicalDOC founded?
faqs.answer: LogicalDOC was founded in 2006 and is headquartered in Carpi, Modena, Italy, with additional offices in the US, Germany, Spain, France, and Portugal.

faqs.question: What's the difference between LogicalDOC Community and Enterprise editions?
faqs.answer: The Community Edition provides core free, open-source document management, while the Enterprise edition adds clustering, advanced workflow automation, deeper records management, and commercial support.

faqs.question: Does LogicalDOC offer cloud hosting?
faqs.answer: Yes, LogicalDOC offers cloud and hybrid-cloud deployment options priced by storage needs and user count, in addition to on-premises Community and Enterprise editions.

faqs.question: Can LogicalDOC run on a NAS device?
faqs.answer: Yes, LogicalDOC offers specialized editions built to run on QNAP and Synology network-attached storage devices.

faqs.question: Who uses LogicalDOC?
faqs.answer: LogicalDOC is used by healthcare organizations, legal firms, construction companies, research institutions, sales and marketing teams, and government agencies with document and records management needs.

use_cases.title: Free self-hosted document management
use_cases.description: Small organizations and technical teams deploy the open-source Community Edition to manage documents without licensing costs.

use_cases.title: Compliance-driven records management
use_cases.description: Regulated industries like healthcare, legal, and government use Enterprise features for formal records retention and workflow compliance.

use_cases.title: NAS-based document management for SMBs
use_cases.description: Small businesses running QNAP or Synology NAS devices add structured document management without a separate server.

features: Open source (LGPL v3) with full source code

enrichment.short_description: No-code governance, risk, and compliance (GRC) platform, Risk Cloud, for building custom risk and compliance workflows.
enrichment.seo_title: LogicGate Review 2026: Features, Pricing and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: LogicGate's Risk Cloud is a no-code GRC platform for risk, compliance, and audit management. Read our 2026 review of features, pricing, and alternatives.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: LogicGate is a governance, risk, and compliance (GRC) software company founded in 2015 in Chicago, Illinois, by co-founders Matt Kunkel (who serves as CEO) and Dan Campbell (CTO), with Jon Siegler as an early co-founder and Chief Product Officer; Kunkel and Siegler had previously worked together as consultants at Navigant Consulting before starting the company. LogicGate's flagship and namesake product is Risk Cloud, a no-code GRC platform that lets organizations build, configure, and automate risk management and compliance workflows without custom software development. Rather than shipping a single fixed application, Risk Cloud is architected around more than 30 purpose-built 'Applications,' each representing a distinct set of workflows aligned to a specific GRC use case or industry framework, covering areas such as enterprise risk management, regulatory compliance management, cyber risk management, third-party (vendor) risk management, policy management, data privacy management, and internal audit. On top of individual applications, LogicGate also offers curated Application Suites for specific verticals and regulatory regimes, including banking, cyber risk, operational risk, and DORA (the EU's Digital Operational Resilience Act) compliance, and it ships pre-built mappings to widely used frameworks and standards such as NIST, ISO 27001, PCI DSS, HIPAA, and GDPR, reducing the setup burden for organizations that need to demonstrate compliance against a specific standard. LogicGate's pricing model is distinctive within the GRC space for how it treats user licensing: rather than charging per named user across an organization, LogicGate's core pricing is based on the number of Applications an organization licenses plus the number of 'Power Users,' meaning the platform administrators who actually build, configure, and manage workflows inside Risk Cloud. Standard end users and 'External Users' (such as third-party vendors filling out a risk questionnaire) are included in the platform at no additional per-seat charge, which is intended to encourage broad internal adoption of workflows without cost scaling directly with headcount. Beyond the base application and Power User licensing, additional cost components can include advanced product add-ons such as Risk Cloud Quantify (a risk quantification module), implementation services to configure applications and build custom dashboards, ongoing professional services and support, and integration services to connect Risk Cloud with other business systems. As is standard for enterprise GRC software, LogicGate does not publish fixed prices or offer self-serve signup; all pricing requires a direct sales conversation and is fully custom based on the applications, user counts, and services an organization needs. LogicGate has built a strong reputation in third-party analyst rankings: it has been named a Leader in the Gartner Magic Quadrant for GRC Tools, is one of only four vendors recognized as a Leader in The Forrester Wave for Governance, Risk, and Compliance Platforms (Q2 2026), and has been named a Leader by G2 for 28 consecutive quarters, reflecting sustained strong customer satisfaction scores relative to competitors. As of mid-2026, employee count estimates from various data providers range from roughly 240 to 300, and the company remains headquartered in Chicago. LogicGate's target audience is mid-market to enterprise organizations, particularly in regulated industries like financial services, banking, healthcare, insurance, and technology, that need to formalize and automate risk assessment, regulatory compliance tracking, vendor/third-party risk management, and internal audit processes, typically managed by dedicated risk, compliance, or GRC teams reporting into a Chief Risk Officer, Chief Compliance Officer, or similar function. Its direct competitors in the GRC platform market include ServiceNow GRC, MetricStream, Diligent (formerly Galvanize/HighBond), OneTrust, Archer (RSA Archer), Vanta and Drata (which lean more toward security/compliance automation for tech companies), and SAI360. LogicGate differentiates itself through its no-code configurability, letting risk and compliance teams build and adapt their own workflows without depending on IT or professional services for every change, its modular 'buy only the applications you need' pricing structure combined with unlimited included standard/external users, and its consistently strong analyst and peer-review recognition (Gartner, Forrester, and G2) as a top-tier player in a market that also includes much larger, broader enterprise software vendors.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: LogicGate is a governance, risk, and compliance (GRC) software company founded in 2015 in Chicago, Illinois, by Matt Kunkel (CEO), Dan Campbell (CTO), and Jon Siegler (CPO). Its flagship product, Risk Cloud, is a no-code platform that lets risk and compliance teams build and automate their own workflows across more than 30 purpose-built Applications.

The company has been recognized as a Leader in the Gartner Magic Quadrant for GRC Tools, as one of four Leaders in The Forrester Wave for GRC Platforms, and has held G2 Leader status for 28 consecutive quarters, reflecting strong market and customer standing.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Risk Cloud offers 30+ purpose-built Applications spanning enterprise risk, regulatory compliance, cyber risk, third-party risk, policy management, data privacy, and internal audit, plus curated Application Suites for banking, cyber, operational risk, and DORA compliance.

The platform ships pre-built mappings to major frameworks like NIST, ISO 27001, PCI DSS, HIPAA, and GDPR, and its no-code configuration engine lets compliance teams adjust workflows without custom development.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: LogicGate uses fully custom, quote-based pricing built around two components: the number of Applications an organization licenses and the number of Power User (administrator) seats; Standard and External users are included at no additional charge.

Additional costs can include advanced modules like Risk Cloud Quantify, implementation services, professional services, and integrations; there is no published price list or self-serve signup, consistent with enterprise GRC software norms.

features.title: No-code workflow builder
features.description: Lets risk and compliance teams build and adjust GRC workflows without custom development.

features.title: 30+ purpose-built Applications
features.description: Modular applications covering enterprise risk, compliance, cyber risk, third-party risk, policy, privacy, and audit use cases.

features.title: Framework mappings
features.description: Pre-built alignment to NIST, ISO 27001, PCI DSS, HIPAA, GDPR, and other standards.

features.title: Application Suites
features.description: Curated bundles for banking, cyber risk, operational risk, and DORA compliance.

features.title: Risk Cloud Quantify
features.description: Advanced add-on module for quantifying risk in financial terms.

features.title: Unlimited standard and external users
features.description: Only platform administrators (Power Users) are licensed; other users are included at no extra cost.

features.title: Third-party risk management
features.description: Dedicated workflows for assessing and monitoring vendor and supplier risk.

features.title: Custom dashboards and reporting
features.description: Configurable reporting and dashboards built during implementation for program visibility.

pros: No-code configurability lets compliance teams adapt workflows without IT dependency
Unique per-Application plus Power-User pricing avoids per-seat costs for standard/external users
Strong, consistent third-party analyst recognition (Gartner, Forrester, G2 Leader for 28 quarters)
Broad library of 30+ pre-built applications and framework mappings reduces setup time
Modular structure lets organizations buy only the applications relevant to their program
cons: Fully custom, opaque pricing with no published rates or self-serve trial
Implementation typically requires professional services, adding to total cost and time
Best suited to mid-market/enterprise budgets, less accessible to small organizations
Advanced features like Risk Cloud Quantify are additional paid add-ons
Evaluating true total cost requires a sales conversation across applications, users, and services

faqs.question: What is LogicGate?
faqs.answer: LogicGate is a governance, risk, and compliance (GRC) software company whose flagship product, Risk Cloud, is a no-code platform for building and automating risk and compliance workflows.

faqs.question: How much does LogicGate cost?
faqs.answer: LogicGate uses fully custom, quote-based pricing based on the number of Applications licensed and the number of Power User (administrator) seats; there is no published price list.

faqs.question: Who founded LogicGate?
faqs.answer: LogicGate was founded in 2015 in Chicago by Matt Kunkel (CEO), Dan Campbell (CTO), and Jon Siegler (CPO).

faqs.question: Does LogicGate charge per user?
faqs.answer: No. LogicGate only charges for Power User (administrator) licenses; Standard and External users are included in the platform at no additional cost.

faqs.question: What frameworks does LogicGate support?
faqs.answer: Risk Cloud ships with pre-built mappings to frameworks including NIST, ISO 27001, PCI DSS, HIPAA, GDPR, and offers specialized suites for banking, cyber risk, and DORA compliance.

faqs.question: Is LogicGate a no-code platform?
faqs.answer: Yes, Risk Cloud is built around a no-code workflow configuration engine that lets risk and compliance teams build and adjust their own applications without custom software development.
